syncInfo.intro: Pro backs up local training data to the cloud and restores it on a new device.
syncInfo.scopeScores: Six-axis scores and merged radar results
syncInfo.scopeInputs: Assessment input drafts (strength, cardio, explosive, muscle, grip, arm size, FFMI, etc.)
syncInfo.scopeProfile: Home body profile and public ladder identity
syncInfo.backupNote: "Backup to Cloud Now" uploads your current local data to your signed-in Google account.
syncInfo.restoreWarning: "Restore from Cloud" overwrites local scores and history with the cloud snapshot — confirm before proceeding.
syncInfo.requirements: Requires Google sign-in (non-anonymous) and an active Pro subscription.

auth.title: Sign In Required
auth.body: Sign in with Google to back up and restore data.

pro.title: Cloud Sync Needs Pro
pro.body: Upgrade to Pro for cloud backup and restore.

oneRm.title: 1RM Calculator
oneRm.subtitle: Enter weight and reps. Estimate one-rep max.
oneRm.hint: Higher-rep sets increase estimation error — prefer lower-rep near-failure sets.

plates.title: Plate Calculator
plates.subtitle: Enter target total and bar weight. Get per-side plate layout.
plates.inputHint: Enter valid target — total must exceed barbell weight.
plates.leftoverWarning: Plate set cannot match exactly; {{value}} {{unit}} remains per side.

plates.title: Per-Side Plate Layout
plates.leftoverWarning: Cannot match exactly; {{value}} {{unit}} remains per side.

sync.backupFail: Backup failed — check network and retry.
sync.unavailable: Cloud sync unavailable. Try again later.
sync.permissionDenied: Cloud sync failed. Try again later.
sync.authFail: Cloud sync requires signed-in Google account (non-anonymous).
sync.restoreOk: Local scores and history replaced from cloud snapshot.
sync.restoreFail: Restore failed — check network and retry.

somatotypeLab.title: Somatotype Geometry Chart
somatotypeLab.emptyHint: Enter height, weight, body fat, wrist, and flexed arm girth within valid ranges, then run geometry analysis.
somatotypeLab.atypicalWarning: Your height-weight ratio is outside the usual Reuleaux band (HWR < 35 or > 50). The chart still renders, but treat coordinates as approximate.
somatotypeLab.legendaryNotice: System Notice: Your current physical development has exceeded the skeletal geometry estimate. Legendary physique mode is unlocked; manual calibration is redundant.

chart.aria: Heath–Carter somatochart: red current point, green max-tuned point

gap.title: Geometry ID: {{height}}cm / {{wrist}}cm
gap.upgradeGuide: Upgrade guide: You still have {{armGap}}cm of arm girth headroom, {{smmGap}}kg of pure skeletal-muscle growth potential, and {{weightGap}}kg of body-weight adjustment room to your physical ceiling.
gap.upgradeGuideAtCeiling: Upgrade guide: Weight, arm girth, skeletal muscle, and body-fat targets are aligned with your current physical ceiling.

appendix.title: Heath–Carter Somatotype Algorithm Guide
appendix.heathCarter: This assessment is built on the classic Heath–Carter somatotype model. The full clinical formula requires multiple skinfolds and biepicondylar bone breadths; to keep field measurement practical, this app decouples that path and uses wrist girth plus body-composition inputs as the algebraic proxy for skeletal frame. That simplification may introduce roughly ±2% to ±4% estimation residual.
appendix.legendaryDefense: [Extreme Morphotype Correction] The default theoretical ceiling reflects the upper bound of lean tissue under a population-normal genetic distribution. When long-term high-intensity strength training has already pushed measured muscle mass and arm girth beyond that civilian estimate, the system activates Legendary Physique Defense: it re-anchors and dynamically expands the ceiling from the athlete’s live metrics to honor physiological supercompensation.
appendix.biaDisclaimer: [Measurement Error & Variability] Skeletal-muscle conversion is aligned to the commercial high-end BIA convention of 0.57 × FFM. Because BIA and this model are sensitive to body-water status, readings taken during high-carbohydrate loading, post-pump states, creatine use, or inflammatory fluid retention may swing by roughly ±3% to ±5%. All values are for physique tracking and scientific reference only and do not constitute medical diagnosis.
